clinical_information.diagnosis: Philadelphia chromosome–positive B-cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia (Ph+ B-ALL), relapsed/refractory
clinical_information.prior_treatments_and_results: Induction (4/1/24-4/29/24): vincristine (weekly), dexamethasone (daily x28 days), L-asparaginase (alternate days), daunorubicin (single dose week 1), imatinib (daily). Consolidation (5/6/24-7/1/24): high-dose methotrexate (weekly), mercaptopurine (daily), vincristine (biweekly), cyclophosphamide (week 3), doxorubicin (final week), imatinib (daily). Maintenance initiated 7/8/24: methotrexate (weekly), mercaptopurine (daily), vincristine (q4 weeks), imatinib (daily); intrathecal methotrexate planned q2-3 months with first dose scheduled 10/4/24. Bone marrow aspirate 3/29/24: 85% lymphoblasts confirming B-ALL. Repeat bone marrow 10/4/24 after consolidation: >90% blasts, consistent with relapsed or refractory disease.
clinical_information.lab_summary: Peripheral CBC values are within reported ranges except for mild basophilia (2%). Bone marrow biopsy is markedly abnormal: ~90% hypercellularity with >90% lymphoid blasts consistent with relapsed/refractory B‑cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia (B‑ALL). CMP, ESR, CRP, and fecal markers were not provided.
clinical_information.symptom_severity_and_impact: Clinically the patient is overall well appearing with only mild fatigue and decreased energy reported by parents; no recent fevers or other systemic symptoms documented. Despite limited subjective symptoms, objective disease burden is high (>90% marrow blasts) indicating significant hematologic malignancy.
clinical_information.prognosis_and_risk_if_not_approved: Relapsed/refractory Ph+ B-ALL with very high marrow blast burden carries high risk of rapid disease progression, marrow failure (cytopenias), life-threatening infection and bleeding, and poor overall prognosis without effective salvage therapy. Delay in appropriate therapy reduces chance of achieving remission and curative options (e.g., subsequent transplant) and increases morbidity and mortality.
clinical_information.clinical_rationale_for_urgency: Repeat bone marrow after completion of consolidation demonstrates >90% blasts indicating relapse/refractory disease despite standard induction/consolidation including imatinib. This high disease burden necessitates prompt initiation of salvage therapy (blinatumomab requested) to attempt remission and prevent rapid clinical deterioration.

treatment_request.rationale: Diagnosis of B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL); Induction cycle 1 (Days 1-28: 28 mcg/day). Patient has received 1 standard induction and consolidation cycle, now in maintenance phase. Please see attached clinical documentation, laboratory results and pathology reports.

Clinical Policy: Blinatumomab (Blincyto)  
Reference N umber: ERX.SPA. 241 
Effective Date: 09 .01.18 
Last Review Date: 08.18  Revision Log  
 
See Important Reminder  at the end of this policy for important regulatory and legal information.  
 
Description 
Blinatumomab (Blincyto®) is a bispecific CD19- directed CD3 T -cell engager that binds to CD19  
(expressed on cells of B -lineage origin) and CD3 (expressed on T cells).  
 
FDA Approved Indication(s) 
Blincyto is indicated for :  
• MRD -positive B -cell precursor ALL  
o Treatment of B -cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL) in first or second complete 
remission with minimal residual disease (MRD) greater than or equal to 0.1% in adults and 
children. This indication is approved under accelerated approval based on MRD response rate and hematological relapse- free survival. Continued approval for this indication may be contingent 
upon verification and description of clinical benefit in the confirmatory trials.  
• Relapsed or refractory B -cell precursor ALL  
o Treatment of relapsed or refractory B -cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL) in adults 
and children.  
 
Policy/Criteria

B -cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL) in adults 
and children.  
 
Policy/Criteria 
Provider must submit documentation (such as office chart notes, lab results or other clinical information) 
supporting that member has met all approval criteria.  
 
It is the policy of health plans affiliated with Envolve Pharmacy Solutions™ that Blincyto is  medically 
necessary when the following criteria are met:  
 I. Initial Approval Criteria  
A. Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia (must meet all) : 
1. Diagnosis of B -cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia (B -ALL);  
2. Prescribed by or in consultation with an oncologist  or hematologist ; 
3. Requested as treatment for (a or b):  
a. B-ALL in remission but positive for minimal residual disease (MRD+);  
b. Relapsed or refractory B -ALL (i and ii):  
i. Philadelphia chromosome- negative (Ph- ) disease;  
ii. Philadelphia chromosome- positive (Ph+) disease and intolerant or refractory to at 
least one second- generation or later tyrosine kinase inhibitor (TKI; i.e., Sprycel
®, 
Tasigna®, Bosulif®, Iclusig®); 
*Prior authorization is (or may be) required for these agents  
4. Dose does  not exceed 28 mcg/day.  
Approval duration:

decision.reason: The request is for blinatumomab for relapsed/refractory Philadelphia chromosome–positive (Ph+) B-ALL. Policy requires that for Ph+ disease the patient be intolerant or refractory to at least one second-generation or later tyrosine kinase inhibitor (e.g., Sprycel, Tasigna, Bosulif, Iclusig). The submitted clinical information documents prior imatinib (first-generation) but does not document use of, intolerance to, or failure of any second- or third-generation TKI. Per the policy I cannot assume such therapy was tried. Please provide documentation of intolerance or treatment failure to at least one second-generation or later TKI (e.g., dasatinib, nilotinib, bosutinib, ponatinib) or other justification meeting policy criteria.
